qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check
description:
Fix documentation inaccuracies that cause first-time plugin developers to hit errors. The docs reference `npx create-opentabs-plugin` which returns a 404 (the package is scoped as @opentabs-dev/create-plugin), and the resources/prompts guide doesn't clearly explain the `override` requirement.

Known LOW-severity issues (not addressed here):
- Inconsistent use of `override` on abstract properties across docs pages (some pages use it on `name`, others don't — both are valid TypeScript since abstract members don't require `override`)

userStories.title: Fix npx create-opentabs-plugin references in docs to use working commands
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolding commands in the docs to actually work when I copy-paste them, so that I don't hit a confusing npm 404 error on my first try.
acceptanceCriteria: All instances of `npx create-opentabs-plugin` in the docs are replaced with a working alternative
The working alternative is `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` (the actual scoped package name)
docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx line 14 is updated
docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx lines 393-396 are updated
docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx line 89 is updated
The docs still note `opentabs plugin create` as the alternative (it already does)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
There are 3 files that reference `npx create-opentabs-plugin`:

1. `docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx` line 14:
   ```
   npx create-opentabs-plugin my-app --domain .example.com --display "My App"
   ```
   Change to: `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin my-app --domain .example.com --display "My App"`

2. `docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx` lines 393-396:
   ```
   The standalone `create-opentabs-plugin` package provides the same functionality:
   npx create-opentabs-plugin my-plugin --domain .example.com --display "My App"
   ```
   Change to: `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin my-plugin --domain .example.com --display "My App"`
   Also update the descriptive text to reference the correct package name.

3. `docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx` line 89:
   ```
   <CliCommand npmCommand="npx create-opentabs-plugin my-plugin" />
   ```
   Change to: `<CliCommand npmCommand="npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin my-plugin" />`

The reason `npx create-opentabs-plugin` doesn't work: npm's `npx` resolves packages by package name, not binary name. The package is `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin` with binary `create-opentabs-plugin`. Only `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` works.

userStories.title: Add override keyword guidance for resources and prompts in docs
userStories.description: As a plugin developer adding resources or prompts to my plugin, I want the documentation to clearly show that `override` is required on the `resources` and `prompts` properties, so that I don't get a confusing TS4114 error.
acceptanceCriteria: The resources-prompts guide explicitly mentions that `override` is required when adding resources or prompts
The plugin-development guide's 'Understand the Plugin Class' section mentions that resources and prompts need `override` (since they have default values on the base class)
The first-plugin tutorial mentions resources/prompts exist and links to the resources-prompts guide with a note about `override`
Code examples in the docs consistently use `override` on resources and prompts
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
The `resources` and `prompts` properties are declared as optional with default values on the `OpenTabsPlugin` base class (in `platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts`):
```typescript
readonly resources?: ResourceDefinition[] = undefined;
readonly prompts?: PromptDefinition[] = undefined;
```

Because they have defaults (not abstract), TypeScript's `noImplicitOverride` requires the `override` keyword when a subclass provides them. The `tools` property is abstract and doesn't need `override`.

Places to update:

1. `docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx` — The 'Registering Resources' (line 153) and 'Registering Prompts' (line 369) sections already show `override` in the code, but there's no explanatory text saying WHY it's needed. Add a short note like: 'Note: `override` is required because `resources` is an optional property with a default value on the base class.'

2. `docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx` — The 'Understand the Plugin Class' section (around line 71) shows the class structure but only mentions tools. Add a brief note about resources and prompts needing `override`.

3. `docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx` — Near the end, after the working example, add a 'Next steps' callout mentioning resources and prompts with a link to the guide.
